home.bio: I'm Alexis. I work in monitoring and evaluation for FAO across Latin America and the Caribbean. I'm doing my PhD in territorial sustainability and I love photography. Here I share my research and some images along the way.
home.research_intro: Research is at the heart of my work. Here you'll find updates on my doctoral thesis, the studies I supervise, and other publications I've developed throughout my career.
home.photography_intro: Photography is one of my greatest passions. I love capturing landscapes, moments and places I come across along the way — whether working in the field for FAO or exploring on my own. This is a small selection of my favourite shots.
home.contact_intro: If you'd like to chat about research, sustainability, development projects or just say hello, feel free to reach out. I'm always open to new conversations and collaborations.

photography.title: Photography
photography.subtitle: A selection of images along the way

education.degree: Master in Strategies and Technologies for Development

experience.organization: FAO — Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
experience.description: Supporting programs and projects of the Emergency and Resilience team in Latin America and the Caribbean. Building tools and methodologies for Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability and Learning.
Agricultural Economics course at the Faculty of Agricultural Sciences.
National coordinator for the M&E system implementation for IOM, ECW-UNICEF and ECHO programs in Ecuador.
National coordinator for the M&E system of the binational Colombia-Ecuador project in coordination with UNHCR and UNICEF.
National coordination of the M&E system and technical-operational advisory for humanitarian assistance projects.
Management and support to national sub-offices. Recovery, protection and Family Farming integration projects.
